title: Staining Unfixed Labyrinthulomycetes with Nile Red
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ff9jq","text":"This protocol modified from: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"avok","text":"Pandey A, Bhathena Z. 2014. Prevalence of PUFA Rich Thraustochytrids sps. along the\nCoast of Mumbai for Production of Bio Oil. Journal of Food and Nutrition\nResearch 2(12): 993-999.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Primary Nile Red (NR) stock: 1mg/mL in DMSO. Working NR stock: 100 µg/mL in DMSO. *Keep NR in the dark.* *NR is not very soluble in water* We have used this protocol successfully for Schizochytrium 28209 grown in 1/2 790 and Artemia soup. We've even seen stained zoospores still swimming! ATCC Medium: 790 By+ Medium Yeast Extract..............................................1.0 g Peptone......................................................1.0 g D+-Glucose.................................................5.0 g Seawater...................................................1000 ml https://www.atcc.org/~/media/920FDAC93FF84B79851C29FBB8049862.ashx 'Artemia Soup' medium 1.25 g ground, freeze-dried brine shrimp autoclaved in 1 liter artifical seawater
Add 10 μl of NR working stock to 100 μl of cell culture. Final staining condition: 10 µg/mL in 10% DMSO.
Cover test tube (wrap in aluminum foil) to prevent NR light exposure and then vortex for 1 minute.
Let stained sample incubate in the dark at room temperature for 5 minutes.
After 5 minutes, mount cells on slide and place cover slip.
The photo shown was taken with a Lietz I3 filter cube (excitation BP 450-490 nm; dichroic 510 nm; emission LP 515).
